headlines.title: Illinois Legislature passes historic AI bill that would require third-party safety audits
headlines.description: The measure, SB315, goes beyond legislation from California and New York regulating America’s largest AI companies. It still needs to be signed by the governor.

headlines.title: Red-hot AI demand pushes more companies into the $1 trillion club
headlines.description: Micron Technologies and SK Hynix joined the trillion-dollar club, which includes Apple, Meta and Tesla.

headlines.title: Gavin Newsom takes a populist turn on AI ahead of a possible 2028 presidential run
headlines.description: AI is becoming a dominant political issue, with many on the right and the left coalescing around concerns about the rapid growth of the industry.

headlines.title: ‘60 Minutes’ journalist says CBS contract ended after furor over delayed segment
headlines.description: CBS has reportedly declined to renew a contract with Sharyn Alfonsi, a “60 Minutes” journalist whose segment about an El Salvadoran prison was abruptly nixed.

headlines.title: Judge declines to jail teenager accused of killing stepsister aboard cruise ship
headlines.description: A federal judge declined to jail a Florida teenager accused of killing and sexually assaulting his stepsister, allowing him to remain in the custody of a family member while he awaits trial.

headlines.title: Graphic: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
headlines.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.

headlines.title: Americans Face Expensive Summer on the Roads and in the Skies
headlines.description: Millions of Americans are gearing up for summer vacation season by hitting the roads and packing airports but those vacations are coming with a much higher price tag. The war in Iran and the disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz are mostly to blame and even if the conflict ends soon, the summer’s travel bill is not expected to go down right away. NBC’s Allie Canal reports in this week’s Sunday Focus.

headlines.title: Expert Guide to a No-Fee Summer: Checked Bags, Rental Cars
headlines.description: From rising baggage fees to surprise hotel charges, summer costs can add up quickly so NBC’s chief investigative correspondent Vicky Nguyen joins TODAY with a guide to a no-fee summer. Her tips include using compression packing cubes to avoid checking a bag, taking a ride-share to a rental car location instead of renting from the airport, and checking credit card perks when booking hotels.

headlines.title: Elon Musk Poised to Be World’s First Trillionaire After SpaceX IPO
headlines.description: Elon Musk is poised to become the world’s first trillionaire after his company, SpaceX, confirmed plans to go public. Because Musk owns the majority of the shares, it could push his net worth over the trillion dollar mark.

headlines.title: Life Lessons for Recent Grads, Anyone Seeking Career Change
headlines.description: Whether you are a recent graduate or someone simply looking for a change, Jodi Kantor, author of the book, “How to Start: Discovering Your Life’s Work” joins TODAY to share life lessons about carving out a fulfilling career. She breaks down how to turn passion into a job by pairing a craft with a need, shares how to combat the existential threat of AI, and gives advice for managing parents' expectations with individual dreams.

headlines.title: How grocery prices have changed since Trump took office
headlines.description: Learn where grocery prices stand during the Trump administration. See how inflation is affecting the cost of eggs, bread, chicken, ground beef and more.

headlines.title: ‘Get in loser, we’re buying Spirit’: One man’s viral plan to revive the airline
headlines.description: Spirit Airlines was the budget-friendly carrier America loved to hate.
